title: C1: Stop discarding compile.Compile() errors in mode handlers
description: All three mode handlers (runExplore, runBuild, runShip) discard the error return from compile.Compile(). If module loading or dependency resolution fails, the program silently produces empty/partial output. Fix: check error at main.go:111,178,245 and call dief.
close_reason: Already fixed by I1 agent - all 3 compile.Compile calls now check errors

title: I1: Change interface{} error returns to error type
description:
## Problem

5 function signatures return errors as `interface{}` instead of `error`:

1. `internal/compile/compile.go:16` — `Compile(...)(string, CompileMeta, interface{})`
2. `internal/loader/load.go:32` — `LoadModules(...)(map, interface{})`
3. `internal/loader/load.go:66` — `LoadRules(...)(..., interface{})`
4. `internal/resolver/requires.go:14` — `ExpandRequires(...)([], map, interface{})`
5. `internal/resolver/requires.go:28-29` — inner `dfs` closure returns `interface{}`

## Impact

`internal/lint/lint.go:105` does `errIf.(error)` which **will panic on typed nil** since nil interface{} is not nil when typed.

## Fix

Change all signatures to return `error`. Update all callers. The lint.go type assertion becomes a simple nil check.

## Files

- internal/compile/compile.go
- internal/loader/load.go
- internal/resolver/requires.go
- internal/lint/lint.go
- cmd/build-prompt/main.go (callers)

title: C3: ListMarkdownFiles silently ignores WalkDir errors
description:
## Problem

`internal/loader/load.go:18` — the WalkDir callback returns nil for all errors, silently skipping files that can't be read (permission denied, symlink loops, etc.).

## Fix

Return the WalkDir error (or wrap it with context about the path) so callers can handle it. Add a test for permission-denied / missing-directory scenarios.

## Files

- internal/loader/load.go:18
close_reason: Already fixed by I1 agent - WalkDir callback now propagates filesystem errors

title: C2: Propagate errors in validateExclusiveGroups and profile loading
description:
## Problem

Three locations silently swallow errors from `LoadModules`/`LoadRules`:

1. `cmd/build-prompt/config.go:101` — `validateExclusiveGroups` discards errors
2. `internal/profile/merge.go:44` — `loader.LoadRules` error discarded
3. `internal/profile/merge.go:73` — `loader.LoadModules` error discarded

## Impact

If prompts directory is missing/malformed, validation passes vacuously — defeats purpose of exclusive group checking.

## Fix

Propagate errors to callers. If `merge.go` is dead code (see I6), just fix config.go.

## Files

- cmd/build-prompt/config.go:101
- internal/profile/merge.go:44,73

title: Bead 3: Persistent lint configuration in rules.yml
description:
## Summary

Move lint defaults from CLI-only to file-based, with CLI flags as override layer.

## rules.yml shape

```yaml
exclusive_groups:
  - risk
  - tone
  - output

lint:
  max_words: 2000
  max_lines: 500
  max_modules: 30
  max_module_words: 500
  max_depth: 4
  require_tags: ["risk:*"]
  forbid_tags: []
  require_fields: ["desc"]
  forbid_empty_body: true
```

## Implementation

1. Add `LintConfig` struct to `model/types.go` mirroring `lint.Config` but with YAML tags
2. Extend `model.Rules` with `Lint LintConfig`
3. `LoadRules()` already deserializes `rules.yml` — struct extension handles it automatically
4. In `main.go` lint case: call `LoadRules()`, extract `LintConfig`, merge with CLI flags (CLI wins when non-zero)
5. If `rules.yml` has no `lint:` section, all values are zero/disabled — identical to current behavior

## Merge rule

CLI flag > rules.yml > zero (disabled). A zero CLI value means "use file default".

## Files

- `internal/model/types.go` — add LintConfig struct, extend Rules
- `cmd/build-prompt/main.go` — load lint config from LoadRules(), merge with CLI Config
- `internal/lint/lint.go` — minor merge helper if needed

## Constraints

- No breaking changes — `ppc lint` without a `lint:` section works exactly as before
- CLI flags must override file-based defaults

title: Add --guardrails CLI flag
description: Add --guardrails flag to build-prompt CLI

title: Write guardrail module files
description: Create directory prompts/guardrails/ with four markdown modules

title: Add guardrails layer to LayerOrder
description: Add guardrails as layer 5 (after contracts) in internal/model/types.go

title: I2: Collapse runExplore/runBuild/runShip into single parameterized function
description:
## Problem

Three mode handlers in `cmd/build-prompt/main.go` (lines 62-261) are nearly identical (~200 lines duplicated). Only difference is the mode string (\explore\, \build\, \ship\).

## Fix

Replace with a single `runMode(mode string, args []string, promptsDir string) int` function. Keep the switch-case in main that calls it.

## Files

- cmd/build-prompt/main.go

title: I5: Consolidate exclusive group validation into single implementation
description:
## Problem

Exclusive group validation implemented 3 times with subtly different approaches:

1. `cmd/build-prompt/config.go:100-141` — loads modules itself, swallows errors
2. `internal/profile/merge.go:72-103` — loads modules itself, swallows errors
3. `internal/resolver/ordering.go:12-45` — `ValidateExclusiveGroups(rules, mods)` operates on pre-loaded data

## Fix

Keep `internal/resolver/ordering.go` version as canonical. Remove the duplicates in config.go and merge.go. Callers should load modules first, then call the canonical function.

## Files

- cmd/build-prompt/config.go:100-141
- internal/profile/merge.go:72-103
- internal/resolver/ordering.go:12-45

title: I3: Consolidate duplicated cycle detection algorithm
description:
## Problem

Same DFS cycle detection implemented twice:

1. `internal/doctor/doctor.go:61-99` — `visit` closure with unvisited/visiting/done state machine
2. `internal/resolver/requires.go:15-76` — identical DFS with same state machine

Bug fixes to one won't propagate to the other.

## Fix

Extract cycle detection into a shared function in `internal/resolver` (or a new shared package). Have `doctor` import and use it.

## Files

- internal/doctor/doctor.go:61-99
- internal/resolver/requires.go:15-76

title: I7: Add unit tests for compile, loader, resolver, render packages
description:
## Problem

9 of 13 packages have zero tests. The core compilation pipeline (`compile`, `loader`, `resolver`) has no unit tests at all. Error paths are completely untested.

## Scope

### `internal/loader/` (priority: highest)
- LoadModules with missing/malformed directories
- ParseFrontmatter with edge cases (empty YAML, Windows line endings, missing delimiters)
- LoadRules with invalid YAML

### `internal/compile/` (priority: highest)
- Compile with missing modules, invalid rules, tag conflicts
- Compile with circular dependencies (should propagate resolver error)

### `internal/resolver/` (priority: high)
- ExpandRequires with cycles, missing deps, deep chains
- ValidateExclusiveGroups with conflicting tags
- OrderModules with complex dependency graphs

### `internal/render/` (priority: medium)
- Render with empty module list, single module, multiple layers

### `internal/profile/` (priority: medium)
- LoadProfile with missing/invalid profiles

### `cmd/build-prompt/` (priority: lower)
- Config parsing and CLI override logic

## Style

Use table-driven tests with t.Run subtests. Use testdata fixtures consistent with existing patterns.

title: I4: Consolidate duplicated layer-name-from-index functions
description:
## Problem

Three functions that map layer index to layer name, each implemented independently:

1. `internal/doctor/report.go:107-122` — `layerNameFromIndex()`
2. `internal/graph/dot.go:126-133` — `clusterNameFromLayer()` + `layerLabelFromIndex()`

`internal/model/types.go:67` already has `LayerOrder`.

## Fix

Add a `LayerName(index int) string` function to `internal/model/types.go`. Replace all three duplicate implementations with calls to it.

## Files

- internal/model/types.go
- internal/doctor/report.go:107-122
- internal/graph/dot.go:126-133

title: I6: Remove dead code in profile package
description:
## Problem

The following are never called from any code:

- `internal/profile/merge.go` — `Merge()`, `MergeOptions`, and internal `validateExclusiveGroups`
- `internal/profile/loader.go` — `LoadProfileFromFile()` (only called by `LoadProfile` in same file, but exported unnecessarily)

## Fix

Remove `merge.go` entirely if I5 consolidates validation. Unexport `LoadProfileFromFile` → `loadProfileFromFile` or inline it.

## Files

- internal/profile/merge.go
- internal/profile/loader.go
close_reason: Already done by C2+I5 agent - merge.go deleted, dead code removed

title: go-standards-audit
description: Align codebase with Go 1.22+ best practices: fix correctness bugs, eliminate duplication, improve error handling, add tests for core packages, and improve tooling.
close_reason: All 14 tasks completed across 5 phases: 4 critical bugs fixed, 5 duplication issues resolved, 73 new test cases added, golangci-lint config created, CI fixed, code cleanup done

title: Bead 2: Path-scoped lint rule targeting
description:
## Summary

Allow lint rules to be targeted at specific module paths using glob patterns. Inspired by conduct's `files:` scoping.

## rules.yml shape

```yaml
lint:
  scopes:
    - paths: ["traits/**"]
      max_module_words: 500
    - paths: ["contracts/**"]
      max_module_words: 2000
  max_words: 2000
```

## Implementation

1. Add `LintScope` struct: `Paths []string` + nullable overrides for per-module thresholds (`MaxModuleWords *int`, `ForbidEmptyBody *bool`, etc.)
2. Path matching: simple glob (`*` for single segment, `**` for any depth) — no full gitignore
3. First matching scope wins (sort scopes lexicographically by first path for determinism)
4. Global `lint:` values are the floor; scoped values override per-module
5. A module not matched by any scope uses global values only

## Scope applicability

Scopes only apply to **per-module** checks:
- max_module_words
- forbid_content (Bead 1)
- forbid_empty_body
- require_fields
- forbid_tags

**Global** checks remain global (they don't make sense per-path):
- max_words, max_lines, max_modules
- require_tags, max_depth

## Files

- `internal/model/types.go` — add LintScope struct
- `internal/lint/lint.go` — scope matching + per-module override logic
- `internal/lint/lint_test.go` — scope matching tests

## Dependencies

Requires: ppc-0dy.1 (persistent lint config infrastructure)

title: I9: Fix coalesceInt to distinguish unset from explicitly-zero CLI flags
description: internal/lint/lint.go:88-93 - coalesceInt uses cli != 0 to check if CLI value was set. This means --max-words 0 (no limit) falls through to the file value. Fix: accept a bool cliSet parameter or use *int for CLI values. Update callers in main.go.

title: I8: Improve SrcError with Unwrap and contextual Error
description: internal/error/types.go - SrcError does not implement fmt.Unwrap(), so wrapped errors lose their chain. Error() returns only e.Msg, discarding Path, ID, and Hint context. Fix: add Unwrap() error method, update Error() to include Path and ID, remove or use the Hint field.

title: S3: Replace log.Printf in substitute with structured warning return
description: internal/substitute/substitute.go:19 calls log.Printf directly to stderr. Library code should not write to stderr. Fix: return unresolved variable paths as part of the function result. Let the caller decide how to report warnings.

title: Bead 1: Content-level lint rules (forbid_content_patterns)
description:
## Summary

Check module body content against regex patterns, scoped by path. Inspired by conduct's `forbidContentPattern`.

## rules.yml shape

```yaml
lint:
  forbid_content_patterns:
    - match: "TODO"
      reason: "no TODOs in modules"
      paths: ["traits/**", "policies/**"]
    - match: "eslint-disable"
      reason: "fix the violation instead"
```

## Implementation

1. Add `LintContentPattern` struct to `model/types.go`: `Match string`, `Reason string`, `Paths []string`
2. Add `ForbidContentPatterns []LintContentPattern` to LintConfig
3. In `lint.Run()`, for each module:
   a. Check if module relative path matches any pattern's `paths` glob (empty paths = match all)
   b. Match body against `regexp.Compile(pattern.Match)`
   c. Fail loudly if regex is invalid
   d. Report violation with `rule: "forbid_content"`, module ID, and reason
4. Add CLI flag `--forbid-content <pattern>` for ad-hoc use (no path scoping, no reason)

## Files

- `internal/model/types.go` — add LintContentPattern struct
- `internal/lint/lint.go` — content pattern checking logic
- `internal/lint/lint_test.go` — pattern matching tests
- `cmd/build-prompt/main.go` — add --forbid-content flag

## Dependencies

Requires: ppc-0dy.2 (path-scoped rule targeting provides the paths: field infrastructure)

title: lint-config-in-rules-yml
Phase 5: Add golangci-lint config and fix CI test coverage gaps
description:
## Scope

### Tooling
1. Add `.golangci.yml` with: errcheck, gosec, revive, govet, staticcheck, unused
2. Add lint target to justfile
3. Remove `dist/` from git, add to `.gitignore`

### CI
4. Fix CI test command to run full suite (`go test ./...`) instead of subset
5. Add missing package-level godoc comments (compile, loader, render, resolver, substitute)

### Minor code cleanup
6. Replace custom `contains()` with `strings.Contains` in lint_test.go
7. Replace custom `min()` with builtin in golden_test.go
8. Remove unused blank import in profile/types.go
9. Make `LayerOrder` unexported or return a copy

## Files

- .github/workflows/lint.yml
- justfile
